entities.description: A framework used to create an environment for actors to engage, experiment, and learn to improve irrigation management

entities.description: The geographical region where the study/application takes place

entities.description: Irrigation systems characterized by low yields and deteriorating infrastructure, behaving as complex socio-ecological systems

entities.description: A state of poor agricultural productivity in small-scale irrigation systems

entities.description: The physical decline of irrigation system components

entities.description: Systems with multiple actors and interconnected subsystems, describing the nature of small-scale irrigation systems

entities.name: ADAPTIVE CAPACITY
entities.description: The ability of irrigation scheme actors to adjust and respond to challenges

entities.name: MARKET-RELATED OFFTAKE
entities.description: The amount of produce sold in the market

entities.name: POVERTY
entities.description: The socio-economic state that actors aim to move out of
